title: unwrap() on regex::Regex::new() in server.rs census block
description: server::serve() builds a Regex with .build().unwrap() (line ~130) for stripping </style> tags from CSS. While the literal regex is always valid, .unwrap() on a Result is a code smell and will panic on any future edit that introduces an invalid pattern. The regex crate provides Regex::new() which returns Result; the idiomatic solution is to use a LazyLock<Regex> (as done in content.rs) so the regex is compiled once at startup and panics at init time with a clear message, rather than mid-request.
close_reason: Duplicate of broadside-zzj3.7 (regex/header .unwrap() on infallible static values).
